title: crataegus monogyna var. macrocarpa
intro: Crataegus monogyna var. macrocarpa, also known as the large-fruited hawthorn, is a variety of the hawthorn tree (Crataegus monogyna), a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which are attributed to its flavonoids, oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs), and other bioactive compounds, that have been traditionally used to support cardiovascular health and lower blood pressure. The health benefits of Crataegus monogyna var. macrocarpa include reducing inflammation, improving circulation, and acting as an antioxidant, making it a popular herbal remedy for heart health and overall well-being. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and capsules, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
